brief_notes: Clear presentation of a biotype-agnostic sRNA fragmentation pipeline with strong benchmark results (F1=95.6%) and compelling multi-mapping read recovery. Strengths: well-motivated problem, clean benchmark against miRNA gold standard, useful cross-species conservation analysis, and good tables. Weaknesses: methods section lacks algorithmic detail (e.g., coverage threshold defaults, multi-mapping graph construction specifics), no runtime/scalability benchmarks, no figures, the conservation analysis relies heavily on existing ortholog annotations without sensitivity analysis, and the fragment calling approach is relatively simple (coverage-based). Citations miss some relevant recent tools. GitHub URL provided but no version tag.
